case_title: TANYA CASTILLO VS. LOUIS VUITTON NORTH AMERICA INC., ET AL

docket_entries.description: MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-21-2017 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LOUIS VUITTON NORTH AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY PROCEEDINGS PENDING ARBITRATION IS GRANTED AND THIS CASE IS STAYED PENDING THE CONCLUSION OF THE ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS. THE COURT DENIES THE REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG; DEPT. 302.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF TANYA CASTILLO'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED AS MOOT. THE ARBITRATOR'S DECISION TO PERMIT MS. CASTILLO TO AMEND HER CLAIM IN ARBITRATION TO INCLUDE THE MATTERS WHICH SHE WANTS TO ADD TO HER COMPLAINT FILED IN THIS CASE MAKES IT UNNECESSARY AND UNWISE FOR THIS COURT TO ENTERTAIN MS. CASTILLO'S MOTION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: NICOLE RODICH, CSR#11604, NRODICH@SFTC.ORG.

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-23-2018 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-13-2017 Continued To Jun-13-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Anup A. Mehta, Esq. of Mehta Law, Tel. 925.750.7961, for plaintiff and moving party;
Raquel M. Mason, Esq. of Winston & Strawn LLP, Tel. 415.591.1567, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. Plaintiff's counsel brought up issues re malpractice and statue of limitations as to new defendant Louis Vuitton USA, Inc. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF TANYA CASTILLO's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint as follows: Plaintiff Tanya Castillo's motion for leave to file first amended complaint is denied as moot. The arbitrator's decision to permit Ms. Castillo to amend her claim in arbitration to include the matters which she wants to add to her complaint filed in this case makes it unnecessary and unwise for this court to entertain Ms. Castillo's motion. Order signed. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Nicole Rodich, CSR#11604, nrodich@sftc.org.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-15-2017 Continued To Nov-29-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Proceedings Pending Arbitration
Off Calendar Mar-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Anup A. Mehta, of Mehta Law, Tel. 925.750.7961, for plaintiffs;
Jennifer N. Zhao, of Winston & Strawn LLP, Tel. 415.591.1432, for defendants.

Ruling: The matter was discussed. DEFENDANT LOUIS VUITTON NORTH AMERICA, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Proceedings Pending Arbitration is granted and this case is stayed pending the conclusion of the arbitration proceedings. The arbitration proceedings will be governed by California law and will be held in San Francisco. Louis Vuitton has shown that plaintiff Tanya Castillo signed the arbitration agreement and that the agreement covers all the claims alleged by Ms. Castillo in her complaint filed on December 30, 2016. While Ms. Castillo has shown that the arbitration agreement is both procedurally and substantively unconscionable, the two substantively unconscionable provisions will be severed. The agreement's procedural unconscionability is established by the fact that it is a contract of adhesion and the manner in which it was given to Ms. Castillo. Its first sentence states that the parties agree to arbitration "as a condition of employment." Ms. Castillo was presented with the agreement in a stack of 10 to 15 documents, told the documents were mandatory, and she was not given an opportunity to read them or informed that one of the documents was an arbitration agreement. The agreement is substantively unconscionable because it contains both forum selection language requiring that the arbitration occur in New York City and choice of law language requiring that New York state law apply. Ms. Castillo's arguments that other provisions of the agreement are substantively unconscionable lack merit. The first two sentences on page 3 of the agreement mandate that, where possible, substantively unconscionable terms should be severed to preserve the parties' intent to arbitrate. Adhering to that language and finding that the substantively unconscionable language can be severed without re-writing the parties' agreement, the forum selection and choice of law language is severed and of no force and effect. Ms. Castillo's objection 3 to the first declaration of Tiffani McDonough is overruled. The evidence submitted with the reply papers was not considered. The Court DENIES the Request for Attorney's Fees. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
